---
title: "Question 2 - Binomial fit and goodness of fit"
author: "Upayan Mazumder"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
alpha <- 0.05
```

## Question

Fit a binomial distribution for the frequency data and test goodness of fit.

```{r data}
# Given distribution
x <- 0:4
obs <- c(5, 20, 45, 20, 10)
N <- sum(obs)

# Since x ranges from 0 to 4, use Binomial(n = 4, p)
n_binom <- 4
mean_x <- sum(x * obs) / N
p_hat <- mean_x / n_binom

expected_prob <- dbinom(x, size = n_binom, prob = p_hat)
expected <- N * expected_prob

fit_table <- data.frame(
  x = x,
  Observed = obs,
  Expected = round(expected, 4),
  Expected_Prob = round(expected_prob, 4)
)

fit_table
p_hat
```

## Goodness-of-fit test

```{r gof-test}
# Pearson chi-square statistic (manual, with parameter-estimation df adjustment)
chi_sq <- sum((obs - expected)^2 / expected)
df <- length(obs) - 1 - 1  # categories - 1 - estimated parameters (p)
p_value <- pchisq(chi_sq, df = df, lower.tail = FALSE)

c(chi_square = chi_sq, df = df, p_value = p_value)
```

```{r decision}
if (p_value < alpha) {
  cat("Decision: Reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: The Binomial(4, p_hat) model is not an adequate fit.")
} else {
  cat("Decision: Fail to reject H0 at 5% significance level.\n")
  cat("Conclusion: The Binomial(4, p_hat) model provides an adequate fit to the data.")
}
```
